title: hibbertia rufa
intro: Hibbertia rufa, also known as the red Guinea flower or yellow bells, is a flowering plant native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hibbertia rufa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Hibbertia rufa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
